spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: autocorrect='on' vs 'off' on input/textarea/contenteditable. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Live interactive controls present and wired up — passes the no-static-card rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and several failure modes are probed, but the page misses a couple of the edge cases the spec calls out (see open questions).

references.rationale: Has a see-also block but no explicit chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link on this concept page — the self-heal slug recovery relies on that link being present.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Probe a real text edit, not just IDL presence
openQuestions.detail: Reflecting the IDL doesn't prove autocorrect is wired through to OS-level autocorrection. A small typeable input that requests focus and reports whether OS suggestions arrive would catch the difference between attribute support and behaviour support.

openQuestions.title: Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page
openQuestions.detail: The self-heal recovery script relies on every concept page carrying the canonical chromestatus link in a see-also or references block. Add it: https://chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336

summary: Live IDL probe + UA report + Safari/Firefox/Chrome support story. Main gaps: Probe a real text edit, not just IDL presence; Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page.
